data.title: Tune Stripe and Printful HTTP clients (timeouts, transport)

data.body:
Severity: performance high (Stripe) / medium (Printful). Source: TYB-12-followup performance audit.

Where: server/internal/stripe/client.go:97-117 (Stripe SDK), server/internal/printful/client.go:114-117 (Printful client).

What: both upstream clients use Go's default http.Transport (MaxIdleConnsPerHost=2). The Stripe SDK doesn't get an explicit *http.Client.Timeout in the production path — only the SDK's internal backend timeout. Two concurrent customers exhaust the idle pool and pay TLS handshakes (~150ms to api.stripe.com / api.printful.com from US east) on every request past the second.

Impact: under any concurrency, both upstream paths inflate by an extra TLS RTT. On the webhook hot path (Stripe ack budget ~10s before retry), GET-then-POST to Printful with handshake overhead can push past 5s — uncomfortable. A pathological Stripe stall holds the user's checkout-start request for the full 60s WriteTimeout.

Recommendation: pass an explicit *http.Client to both:
  &http.Client{
    Timeout: 15 * time.Second,
    Transport: &http.Transport{
      MaxIdleConnsPerHost: 16,
      IdleConnTimeout:     90 * time.Second,
      ForceAttemptHTTP2:   true,
    },
  }
For Stripe, wire via stripego.BackendConfig.HTTPClient for every request, not only when BaseURL is overridden. For Printful, set client.httpClient.Transport in New().

Effort: small. No new deps.
